data.description:
Repro (verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD, temp store): two epic children with identical set-file-impact paths ([{"path":"src/shared.py","reason":"edit"}] on both) -> next-batch puts BOTH in the same batch, "files": [] each, skipped_overlap: [], conflict matrix all '.'. Control: the same backticked path in a COMMENT on each ticket IS detected (SKIPPED_OVERLAP). So the documented contract (CLAUDE.md + set-file-impact help: next-batch uses the recorded {path,reason} array to avoid file-conflicting co-scheduling) is dead.

Source (src/rebar/_engine/ticket-next-batch.sh): file_impact never read (only the unrelated analyze_file_impact helper name matches); :627-634 rebuilds ready_tasks dicts DROPPING description, so :769 raw.get("description") is always None (dead code); :455-481 _load_ticket_body assembles only title + comment bodies.

Fix direction: union set(e["path"]) from raw.get("file_impact", []) into Candidate.files (~:768); carry description + file_impact through the ready_tasks dict at :627.

RED test: two children with identical set-file-impact paths -> exactly one in batch, the other in skipped_overlap.
data.title: next-batch ignores recorded file-impact: conflict detection reads title+comments only
